testingRequirements: Tests must validate user-facing behavior, not implementation details
Focus on what users actually do - integration tests over unit tests
Don't mock excessively - test real behavior where possible
Coverage metrics are meaningless - user flow coverage is everything

userStories.title: Drag-to-reorder projects in the workspace Projects list (reuse @dnd-kit)
acceptanceCriteria: User can grab a project row by its handle and drop it elsewhere; the list reorders immediately (optimistic) and the order persists across reload.
Keyboard reorder works (focus handle → Space to pick up → arrows → Space to drop) with screen-reader announcements.
Clicking a project row (not the handle) still opens the project; the 8px activation constraint prevents accidental drags on click.
Reorder is disabled/ignored while a search filter is active (documented behavior), and re-enabled when the search is cleared.
No layout shift / flicker on drop.
userStories.description:
## Goal

Let the user drag project rows into a custom order on the workspace Projects list (`src/routes/index.tsx`, the "Projects" page from the screenshot), persisting via the reorder hook with instant optimistic feedback.

## Depends on

Schema ticket + reorder API/hook ticket.

## Why this approach

@dnd-kit is already wired for the kanban — we reuse that exact setup (vertical list, fractional-midpoint drop math) so the interaction feels identical and we add no new dependency. The list is a simple vertical list, so this is a straightforward `verticalListSortingStrategy`.

## Reference implementation to copy

- DndContext + sensors + accessibility announcements: `KanbanBoard.tsx:17-28, 54-64, 197-200, 390-397`.
- `SortableContext` with `verticalListSortingStrategy`: `KanbanColumnContent.tsx:155`.
- `useSortable` + `CSS.Transform.toString` + drag handle/listeners: `SortableTicketCard.tsx:32-54`.
- Fractional-midpoint drop math (drop-to-end / before-first / between-two): `KanbanBoard.tsx:286-310`.

## Changes

1. Wrap the project list in `src/routes/index.tsx` with `DndContext` (PointerSensor `{ distance: 8 }` + KeyboardSensor with `sortableKeyboardCoordinates`, plus accessibility `announcements`) and a `SortableContext` over the project IDs using `verticalListSortingStrategy`.
2. Make `ProjectListItem` (`src/components/projects/ProjectListItem.tsx`) sortable via `useSortable({ id: project.id })` — apply transform/transition style, `opacity` while dragging, and add a **visible drag handle** (grip icon) that carries the `listeners` so clicking the card still navigates (don't make the whole card the drag handle — preserve the existing `onClick` to open the project).
3. `onDragEnd`: compute the target `position` with the fractional-midpoint algorithm against the **position-ordered** project array, then call `useUpdateProjectPosition().mutateAsync({ id, position })`.
4. **Search interaction**: dragging should be disabled (or no-op for persistence) while a search filter is active, since reordering a filtered subset is ambiguous. Simplest correct behavior: only enable drag when the search box is empty; document this choice.

## Acceptance criteria

- User can grab a project row by its handle and drop it elsewhere; the list reorders immediately (optimistic) and the order persists across reload.
- Keyboard reorder works (focus handle → Space to pick up → arrows → Space to drop) with screen-reader announcements.
- Clicking a project row (not the handle) still opens the project; the 8px activation constraint prevents accidental drags on click.
- Reorder is disabled/ignored while a search filter is active (documented behavior), and re-enabled when the search is cleared.
- No layout shift / flicker on drop.

## Validation

- `pnpm check` passes.
- `pnpm build` (touches a route/component) and **`pnpm build:analyze`** — this changes the `/` route's chunk graph by pulling @dnd-kit in. Record before/after initial-load bundle numbers per `docs/performance/validating-optimizations.md`; lazy-load if it regresses the home route meaningfully, and ratchet `docs/performance/perf-budgets.json` if needed.
- Manually verify in browser at `localhost:4242`: mouse drag, keyboard drag, click-to-open still works, responsive layout.
- Add a test for the observable reorder behavior (user drags row → order changes) following the Kent C. Dodds philosophy.

## Notes

- Reuse, don't fork: if the kanban's sensor option constants or announcement helpers can be lifted to a shared module without churn, prefer that over copy-paste. Otherwise keep it local and consistent.

userStories.title: Propagate custom project order to desktop and mobile sidebars

alternatives: Desktop `ProjectsPanel` receives `projects` from `AppLayout` via `useProjectsWithAIActivity()` (`src/components/AppLayout.tsx:129`), which builds on `useProjects()` → `queryKeys.projectsWithEpics`. Once the server fn orders by `position`, the panel order follows automatically.

Mobile `MobileSidebar` calls `useProjects()` directly (`src/components/MobileSidebar.tsx:51`) and passes straight to `ProjectTree` (`src/components/ProjectTree.tsx:105`) with no sort — so it also follows automatically.

acceptanceCriteria: Reordering projects in the workspace list updates the order shown in the desktop `ProjectsPanel` (after its query refreshes) without a manual refresh beyond normal cache invalidation.
The mobile `ProjectTree` reflects the same custom order.
Search/filter in both sidebars preserves the custom order of the matching subset.
userStories.description:
## Goal

Ensure the custom order set in the Projects list shows up everywhere projects are listed — specifically the desktop `ProjectsPanel` slide-out and the mobile `MobileSidebar`/`ProjectTree`.

## Depends on

Schema ticket (ordered data layer). Largely a verification + small-fix ticket because all consumers read the same query.

## Why this should be cheap

- Desktop `ProjectsPanel` receives `projects` from `AppLayout` via `useProjectsWithAIActivity()` (`src/components/AppLayout.tsx:129`), which builds on `useProjects()` → `queryKeys.projectsWithEpics`. Once the server fn orders by `position`, the panel order follows automatically.
- Mobile `MobileSidebar` calls `useProjects()` directly (`src/components/MobileSidebar.tsx:51`) and passes straight to `ProjectTree` (`src/components/ProjectTree.tsx:105`) with no sort — so it also follows automatically.

## Changes / checks

1. Confirm neither sidebar applies its own project-level `.sort()` that would override `position`. (The desktop panel sorts *epics* newest-first at `ProjectsPanel.tsx:626-628` — that is fine and unrelated; do not touch it.)
2. Verify the desktop panel's `filteredProjects` `useMemo` (`ProjectsPanel.tsx:184-187`) preserves incoming order after filtering (filtering should not re-sort).
3. If any consumer derives its own ordering, remove it so `position` is the single source of truth.

## Acceptance criteria

- Reordering projects in the workspace list updates the order shown in the desktop `ProjectsPanel` (after its query refreshes) without a manual refresh beyond normal cache invalidation.
- The mobile `ProjectTree` reflects the same custom order.
- Search/filter in both sidebars preserves the custom order of the matching subset.

## Validation

- `pnpm check` passes.
- Manual cross-surface check at `localhost:4242`: reorder on the Projects page, open the desktop panel and (responsive/mobile) the mobile sidebar, confirm identical order. Confirm filtering keeps order.
- If a fix was needed, add/adjust a test asserting the rendered sidebar order matches the data order.

userStories.title: Default-sort epics newest-first on the project detail page
acceptanceCriteria: The project detail page lists epics with the most recently created epic at the top.
Creating a new epic makes it appear at the top of the list.
No regression to ticket counts, epic links, or the "Add Epic" affordance.
userStories.description:
## Goal

On the project detail page (`src/routes/projects.$projectId.tsx`, the "EPICS" list from the screenshot), render epics **newest-first** by default instead of the current DB insertion order (oldest-first).

## Why

Currently `getProjectsWithEpics` (`src/api/projects.ts:52-119`) has no `ORDER BY` for epics, so `project.epics` comes back in leftJoin/insertion order (oldest first). The detail page maps it directly (`projects.$projectId.tsx:163-176`) with no sort. Users expect the most recent epic at the top. Note the desktop sidebar already sorts epics newest-first inline (`ProjectsPanel.tsx:626-628`) — this aligns the detail page with that behavior.

## Changes (pick the cleanest single place)

- Preferred: sort `project.epics` newest-first where the detail page consumes it. `epic.createdAt` is an ISO 8601 string, so `[...project.epics].sort((a, b) => b.createdAt.localeCompare(a.createdAt))` (descending) is correct and dependency-free.
- Alternative: add `.orderBy(desc(epics.createdAt))` in `getProjectsWithEpics`. This is also fine and would centralize ordering, but verify it does not disturb the inline sort the desktop panel already does (it would simply make that sort redundant, which is harmless).
- Whichever layer is chosen, keep it to one place — do not sort in multiple layers.

## Acceptance criteria

- The project detail page lists epics with the most recently created epic at the top.
- Creating a new epic makes it appear at the top of the list.
- No regression to ticket counts, epic links, or the "Add Epic" affordance.

## Validation

- `pnpm check` passes.
- Manual check at `localhost:4242`: open a project with several epics (e.g. Brain Dump), confirm newest-first; create an epic and confirm it lands on top.
- Add a small test verifying the rendered epic order is newest-first given known `createdAt` values (test the observable order, not the sort fn).

## Notes

- Independent of the reorder tickets — can ship in parallel.

userStories.title: Add `position` column to projects and order the shared data layer by it
acceptanceCriteria: `projects` table has a non-null `position` column with an index; `pnpm db:generate` + `pnpm db:migrate` apply cleanly on an existing database.
Existing projects retain their current visual order after migration (deterministic backfill, no NULLs).
Newly created projects (via web `createProject` and via MCP/CLI `core/project.ts`) receive `MAX(position)+1`.
`core/db-rows.ts` `DbProjectRow` includes `position`; core read path compiles and returns it.
userStories.description:
## Goal

Give projects a persistent, user-controllable sort order by adding a `position` column, and make every project list read it. This is the foundation the reorder UI and sidebar propagation build on.

## Why this approach

`tickets` and `claudeTasks` already use a `position: real` fractional-ordering column. We mirror that exact pattern for `projects` so the codebase stays consistent and the drop algorithm (next ticket) can be copied verbatim. Because the workspace list and both sidebars all read the **same** `getProjectsWithEpics` query, ordering here propagates everywhere for free.

## Changes

1. **Schema** — `src/lib/schema.ts` (`projects` table, ~line 5):
   - Add `position: real("position").notNull()` (mirror `tickets.position` at `src/lib/schema.ts:46`).
   - Add an index for consistency: `index("idx_projects_position").on(table.position)`.
2. **Migration** — run `pnpm db:generate` (creates a file in `drizzle/`), then `pnpm db:migrate`. The generated SQL must **backfill existing rows** with sequential positions (e.g. ordered by `created_at`/rowid) so current ordering is preserved on upgrade — verify/adjust the generated SQL to set deterministic positions for existing projects rather than leaving them NULL/0.
3. **Initial position on create** — `createProject` in `src/api/projects.ts` (~line 122): assign `MAX(position) + 1` for new projects (mirror `createTicket` at `src/api/tickets.ts:123-130`). Also set position in the `core/` create path (`core/project.ts createProject`) used by the MCP/CLI so projects created outside the web UI still get a valid position.
4. **Order the reads**:
   - `getProjectsWithEpics` (`src/api/projects.ts:52-119`): add `ORDER BY projects.position` to the query so the returned `Array.from(projectMap.values())` is position-ordered. Keep epic ordering untouched (handled in a separate ticket).
   - `getProjects` (`src/api/projects.ts:46`): add the same ordering.
   - `core/project.ts listProjects` currently does `ORDER BY name`. Decide and document: keep alphabetical for MCP, OR switch to `ORDER BY position` for parity. Recommended: order by `position` for parity with the UI; update `DbProjectRow` (`core/db-rows.ts:10-17`) to include `position`.

## Acceptance criteria

- `projects` table has a non-null `position` column with an index; `pnpm db:generate` + `pnpm db:migrate` apply cleanly on an existing database.
- Existing projects retain their current visual order after migration (deterministic backfill, no NULLs).
- Newly created projects (via web `createProject` and via MCP/CLI `core/project.ts`) receive `MAX(position)+1`.
- `getProjectsWithEpics` and `getProjects` return projects ordered by `position`.
- `core/db-rows.ts` `DbProjectRow` includes `position`; core read path compiles and returns it.

## Validation

- `pnpm check` (type-check + lint + test) passes.
- `pnpm db:migrate` against a copy of the dev DB succeeds and preserves order (note before/after row order).
- Add/adjust a focused test for the projects core read path ordering if one exists for that module.

## Notes

- Do NOT add blocking work to hot paths; this is a single indexed column + one `ORDER BY`.
- Take a backup first: `pnpm brain-dump admin backup` before testing the migration.

userStories.title: Add `updateProjectPosition` server fn + optimistic `useUpdateProjectPosition` hook
acceptanceCriteria: Calling `updateProjectPosition` with a valid `{ id, position }` persists the new position; invalid input throws a clear error.
`useUpdateProjectPosition` updates the cache optimistically (list reorders immediately) and rolls back with a visible error if the server call fails.
After settle, the list reflects server truth via invalidation.
userStories.description:
## Goal

Provide the write path for project reordering, with instant optimistic UI, by mirroring the existing ticket-position machinery.

## Depends on

Schema ticket (projects `position` column must exist).

## Why this approach

`updateTicketPosition` + `useUpdateTicketPosition` already implement exactly this: a validated `{ id, position }` server function and an optimistic mutation that patches the query cache and rolls back on error. Copying the pattern keeps behavior and error-handling identical and avoids a bespoke solution.

## Changes

1. **Server function** — `src/api/projects.ts`: add `updateProjectPosition` mirroring `updateTicketPosition` (`src/api/tickets.ts:282-302`):
   - `createServerFn({ method: "POST" })` with `.inputValidator((input: { id: string; position: number }) => ...)` rejecting missing id / non-numeric position.
   - Use `ensureExists(project, "Project", id)`.
   - Update `projects.position` via Drizzle (`db.update(projects).set({ position }).where(eq(projects.id, id))`). Note: `projects` has no `updatedAt` column today — do not set one unless the schema ticket adds it.
   - Return the updated row.
2. **React hook** — `src/lib/hooks/projects.ts`: add `useUpdateProjectPosition` mirroring `useUpdateTicketPosition` (`src/lib/hooks/tickets.ts:366-413`):
   - `onMutate`: cancel in-flight queries for `queryKeys.projectsWithEpics`, snapshot, optimistically patch the matching project's `position` in the cached array, and **re-sort the cached array by position** so the optimistic order matches what the server will return.
   - `onError`: roll back to snapshot + surface a user-visible error (toast) per Rule 1/Rule 2 (no silent failure).
   - `onSettled`: invalidate `queryKeys.projectsWithEpics`.

## Acceptance criteria

- Calling `updateProjectPosition` with a valid `{ id, position }` persists the new position; invalid input throws a clear error.
- `useUpdateProjectPosition` updates the cache optimistically (list reorders immediately) and rolls back with a visible error if the server call fails.
- After settle, the list reflects server truth via invalidation.

## Validation

- `pnpm check` passes.
- Add a test that verifies the optimistic reorder + rollback-on-error user-visible behavior (mock the boundary/server fn, assert the visible order changes then reverts on failure) — follow the project's Kent C. Dodds testing philosophy (test the observable reorder, not internals).

## Notes

- This ticket adds no UI; the drag interaction lives in the next ticket. Keep the hook generic enough to be driven by the @dnd-kit `onDragEnd` handler.

techStack: Database: SQLite with better-sqlite3, Drizzle ORM
State: TanStack Query for server state

dos: Use Drizzle ORM: `db.select().from(tickets)`
Use typed schema imports: `import { tickets } from "../lib/schema"`
Use `eq()`, `and()`, `sql` from drizzle-orm for conditions
Use transactions for multi-table operations: `db.transaction(() => {...})`
Use `.get()` for single row, `.all()` for multiple
donts: Raw SQL strings: `db.run("SELECT * FROM tickets")`
String-based table names
String concatenation for WHERE clauses
Multiple independent queries that should be atomic
Assume query returns what you expect without checking

dos: Use `useQuery` with `queryKeys` for data fetching
Use `useMutation` with `onSuccess` for state changes
Invalidate queries after mutations: `queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: queryKeys.tickets })`
Use centralized `queryKeys` object from `src/lib/hooks.ts`
Create custom hooks in `src/lib/hooks.ts` for reusable query logic
donts: `useState` + `useEffect` for fetched data
Direct API calls without proper cache invalidation
Manual cache manipulation or refetch after every change
Hardcoded query key strings throughout components
Duplicate query setup in multiple components

dos: Use Tailwind classes for static styles: `className="p-4 bg-slate-800"`
Use CSS variables for theming: `var(--bg-primary)`, `var(--shadow-modal)`
Reserve inline styles for dynamic/computed values: `style={{ width: `${percent}%` }}`
Define shared style constants at module level for referential stability
Use `React.CSSProperties` type for inline style objects when needed
donts: Inline style objects for single-use styles
Hardcoded colors that don't adapt to themes
Mix Tailwind and inline styles inconsistently
Create new objects inside components that are reused
Untyped style objects that miss IDE autocomplete

dos: Use `createServerFn({ method: "GET" })` for reads, `"POST"` for writes
Use `.inputValidator()` for type-safe input handling
Return typed data directly: `return db.select().from(tickets).all()`
Use `ensureExists()` for required lookups: `ensureExists(project, "Project")`
Import from `@tanstack/react-start` (not `@tanstack/react-start/server`)
donts: Mix GET/POST inconsistently
Access raw input without validation
Wrap in unnecessary response objects
Return null and let caller handle missing data
Wrong import path

dos: Use Zod schemas for input validation: `{ ticketId: z.string() }`
Return structured `{ content: [{ type: "text", text: ... }] }` format
Set `isError: true` for error responses
Use `log.info()` / `log.error()` from `../lib/logging.js`
Include helpful error messages: `"Project not found. Use list_projects to see available."`
donts: Trust input without validation
Return plain strings
Return error text without the error flag
`console.log` in MCP server code
Generic "Not found" errors

dos: Test user behavior: what users see, click, and experience
Integration tests for workflows: test components together
Real database fixtures with actual schema
Tests that fail when user-facing behavior breaks
Ask: "Does this test catch bugs users would encounter?"
donts: Test implementation details, internal state, or private methods
Unit tests for every function
Excessive mocking of internals
Tests that break on refactoring internals
Chase 100% code coverage as a goal

verificationSteps: Run this project's validation commands (`pnpm check` for Brain Dump itself)
Record exact pass/fail/skipped results in a test_report comment
If no automated validation command is discoverable, record the manual smoke check performed
Added tests for new functionality (ONLY tests that verify real user behavior - see Testing Philosophy above)
Used typed error classes (not generic `Error`)
Used Drizzle ORM (not raw SQL) - see DO/DON'T table above
Followed existing patterns from DO/DON'T tables
No hardcoded values that should be configurable
Existing tests still pass
No regressions in related functionality
Updated tests if behavior changed
Did not break backward compatibility (unless explicitly requested)
Manually verified in browser at `localhost:4242`
Checked responsive layout
Verified TanStack Query invalidates and updates correctly
Accessibility: keyboard navigation works, proper ARIA labels
Migration file created via `pnpm db:generate`
Migration tested with `pnpm db:migrate`
Backup tested if schema changed (use `pnpm brain-dump backup` then test restore)
Updated `src/lib/schema.ts` with proper types and constraints
Tested tool via Claude Code integration
Verified error responses are informative (see DO/DON'T table)
Updated tool documentation if interface changed
Added Zod schema for input validation
Followed the measure → fix → re-measure → prove loop in [`docs/performance/validating-optimizations.md`](docs/performance/validating-optimizations.md)
Ran the matching gate: `pnpm perf:check` (bundle), `pnpm test:perf` (browser flow), and/or `pnpm test:e2e perf-renders` (render counts)
Recorded the before/after result (command + numbers) in the ticket completion summary using the template in the runbook
Ratcheted the relevant ceiling in `docs/performance/perf-budgets.json` if the change locked in a win
All acceptance criteria from ticket met
Work summary added via `comment` tool, `action: "add"` (for Ralph sessions)
Session completed with appropriate outcome (for Ralph sessions)
Committed with proper message format: `feat(<ticket-id>): <description>`

epicDescription:
## Why

The workspace Projects list (`src/routes/index.tsx`) and the project sidebars currently render projects in raw SQLite insertion order — there is no `ORDER BY` at the DB layer, no `.sort()` in any hook, and no sort in the rendering components. Users want to express their own priority by **dragging projects into a custom order** in the Projects list view, and have that order **propagate everywhere projects are listed** (desktop `ProjectsPanel` slide-out and mobile `ProjectTree`).

Separately, on the project detail page (`src/routes/projects.$projectId.tsx`) the EPICS list renders in DB insertion order (oldest-first). Users want it **default-sorted newest-first**.

## Design / Reuse (maintainability first)

Both the workspace list and both sidebars read the **same single TanStack Query cache entry** (`queryKeys.projectsWithEpics` → `getProjectsWithEpics` in `src/api/projects.ts`). Ordering applied once at that data layer therefore propagates to every consumer automatically — no per-component sort needed.

We mirror the **existing ticket reorder system** rather than invent a new one:
- `tickets` already uses a `position: real` column with a **fractional-midpoint** drop algorithm (no renumbering). See `tickets` schema (`src/lib/schema.ts:46`), `updateTicketPosition` server fn (`src/api/tickets.ts:282-302`), `useUpdateTicketPosition` optimistic hook (`src/lib/hooks/tickets.ts:366`), and the drop math in `KanbanBoard.tsx:286-310`.
- @dnd-kit is already a dependency (`@dnd-kit/core`, `/sortable`, `/utilities`) and wired in the kanban (`DndContext` + `SortableContext` + `useSortable` + `PointerSensor`/`KeyboardSensor` + accessibility announcements). The projects list reuses this exact setup.

## Performance / UX

- Local-first → must feel instant. Reorder uses optimistic cache updates (no spinner, no flicker), exactly like the kanban.
- Adding @dnd-kit to the `/` route may shift the initial-load chunk graph; run `pnpm build:analyze` and keep the home route lean (lazy where appropriate). Record before/after numbers.
- Keyboard-accessible drag (KeyboardSensor + announcements) and a clear drag handle.

## Scope (tickets)

1. Schema: add `position` to `projects`, migration + backfill, order the shared data layer (web API **and** `core/` for MCP parity) by position, set initial position on create.
2. API: `updateProjectPosition` server fn + `useUpdateProjectPosition` optimistic hook.
3. UI: drag-to-reorder in the workspace Projects list (reuse the kanban @dnd-kit pattern, keyboard-accessible, drag handle).
4. Propagation: verify/ensure custom order is reflected in desktop `ProjectsPanel` and mobile `ProjectTree`, and that search/filter preserves order.
5. UX: default-sort epics newest-first on the project detail page.

## Out of scope

- Reordering epics or tickets via this feature (tickets already have it; epics get sort-only here).
- Per-user ordering / multi-user (Brain Dump is single-user local-first).
